reviewer: Codex native-size reference and autoplay-clip review

characterCraft.note: At native 160x360, the 17x22 caped hero has a face, hair, separate limbs, boots, sword arm, facing, gait, slash, hurt, collapse, and admire poses. Slime, bat, armored guard, and the singular 29x34 horned warden keep distinct construction and locomotion at reference-game scale; none reads as a board token or enlarged hitbox.

environmentCraft.note: The dungeon reads as a built overhead place with masonry walls, recessed floors, lintels, foreground courses, room thresholds, fogged unknown chambers, and role-specific landmarks. Shelves and astrolabes, forges and chains, sarcophagi and moon dials, then roots and fountains give each floor the prop density and material specificity visible in MACHINE HUNT and BLOCK MINE.

levelVariety.note: The Scriptorium, Ember Forge, Moon Ossuary, and Verdant Reliquary replace wall, floor, landmark, light, ambient-motion, and act silhouettes rather than recoloring one room set. The native montage makes every floor recognizable without its title, and measured adjacent-floor structure distances all clear the executable composition floor.

animationImpact.note: The inspected 30-second autoplay clip shows continuous hero and enemy travel, a readable guard tell, displaced sidestep and blocked-lane brace responses, sword counters, sigil and vault payoffs, the full-screen FLOOR ROUTED apex, and a transition into a materially rebuilt floor. Biome warnings occupy the dungeon before land; tier-three presentation retains exact hitstop, slow motion, admire, physical overlay, and subordinate sim-inert particles.

readability.note: Numbered sigils visibly feed 0/2 and 1/2 vault states; the guard's ring/exclamation tell, charge streak, hero SIDESTEPPING or BLOCK response, and room-changing solve carry intent without a route overlay. Unknown rooms remain fogged, the mini-map reveals only discovered topology, the HUD reports local room/actor state rather than the planner target, and an exact contamination fixture proves target, tactic, route, old-route, and trail buffers cannot change one pixel.

artDirectionCohesion.note: Cream stone highlights, ink recesses, biome accents, gold sigils and rewards, cyan interaction energy, and coral danger form one consistent retro-adventure language across architecture, actors, local-state HUD, enemy tells, and payoffs. The dense tiled craft is distinct from both references while meeting their authored-world floor at the actual 160x360 viewing size.
